name: City Landscape In Sight — Window View Perception
description: Pairwise comparison survey for six window-view perception dimensions (preference, monotony, quietness, extensiveness, vividness, oppressiveness). Replicates the crowdsourced WVI methodology from Peng et al. (2026), Landscape and Urban Planning.
author: Peng et al.

elements.name: wvi_intro
elements.title: About this survey
elements.description: You will see two window-view images side by side. Click the one that better matches the question (e.g. more preferred, more extensive). There is no "equal" option — please choose the image that fits better, even if the difference is small. Upload window-view images from your project media dataset (or link the Hugging Face dataset sijiey/City-Landscape-In-Sight).

pages.name: page_prefer
pages.title: Preference

elements.name: prefer
elements.title: Which window view do you prefer?

pages.description: Which window view do you prefer overall?

pages.name: page_monotonous
pages.title: Monotony

elements.name: monotonous
elements.title: Which window view appears more monotonous?

pages.description: Which window view appears more monotonous (more visually uniform and repetitive)?

pages.name: page_quiet
pages.title: Quietness
pages.description: Which window view appears quieter (more peaceful, less noisy)?

pages.name: page_extensive
pages.title: Extensiveness

elements.name: extensive
elements.title: Which window view offers a more extensive perspective?

pages.description: Which window view offers a more extensive perspective (more open and far-reaching)?

pages.name: page_vivid
pages.title: Vividness

elements.name: vivid
elements.title: Which window view appears more vivid?

pages.description: Which window view appears more vivid (more lively and visually diverse)?

pages.name: page_oppressive
pages.title: Oppressiveness

elements.name: oppressive
elements.title: Which window view appears more oppressive?

pages.description: Which window view appears more oppressive (more enclosed and visually constraining)?

config.title: Window View Perception Survey — City Landscape In Sight
config.description: You will compare pairs of window views from residential apartments. For each question, choose the image that better matches the stated perception dimension. All images depict city landscapes as seen from living-room balcony windows. Based on: Peng, C., Yang, S., Liu, A., Xiang, Y., Zhou, Z., & Biljecki, F. (2026). City landscape in sight: A crowdsourced framework for unlocking urban-scale window view perceptions from real estate imagery. Landscape and Urban Planning, 275, 105734.
